meta.title: ROGS Safety Authorisation — North Vale Light Rail (infrastructure manager)
meta.subtitle: Infrastructure manager → safety authorisation · references the SMS · for submission to ORR
meta.author: HSE Rail
meta.classification: Internal — for submission to ORR / competent-person review required
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label (Accountable Duty-Holder, safety-critical roles, COSS). Identifiers and any Sentinel numbers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document. This application is assembled FOR submission to ORR; granting the authorisation is the Safety Authority's act and is not asserted here.

sections.text: Application by North Vale Light Rail, the mainline infrastructure manager for the North Vale guided-transport corridor. As an infrastructure manager, the ROGS route is a SAFETY AUTHORISATION (not a safety certificate, which a transport operator would file, and not a Part 3 verification, which a non-mainline operation would file). This application is built to the ROGS 2006 (SI 2006/599) requirements and is assembled FOR submission to ORR — it does not assert authorisation by ORR.

Route resolved: an infrastructure manager files a safety AUTHORISATION. A mainline transport operator would file a safety certificate; a non-mainline operation would file a ROGS Part 3 safety verification.

This application REFERENCES North Vale's existing rail Safety Management System (SMS ref NV-SMS v4.2, built via rail-safety-management-system, RAIL-01) as an input — it does NOT rebuild the SMS element set.

The application references the dutyholder's existing SMS (NV-SMS v4.2) covering the North Vale guided-transport infrastructure: track, signalling, and structures. The SMS element set (policy, accountabilities, risk-control arrangements, CSM-RA interface, competence/Sentinel, asset/ECM maintenance, emergency, monitoring/audit, continuous improvement) is held in the SMS and is cited here by reference, not reproduced. Where this application summarises a risk-control, it cites the SMS section it draws on.
3. Risk-control summary (hierarchy of controls applied — SFAIRP)

rows.title: Track-worker exposure to running lines during maintenance
rows.description: Maintenance access to the corridor exposes track workers to running-line movements where separation is not yet engineered.

rows.title: Signalling-asset degradation at the corridor's oldest interlocking
rows.description: An ageing interlocking carries a degraded-mode operating risk.

rows.control: Plan possessions / line-blockages to separate workers from running lines where reasonably practicable
Train-warning / TCOD systems where full separation is impractical; interlocking renewal programme

sections.text: No track-worker task is left to a lookout-only administrative control where a separation or train-warning (higher-order) control is reasonably practicable (SFAIRP). The risk-control summary draws on the SMS; it does not re-author it.

items: A proposed re-signalling of the oldest interlocking is a significant change — the CSM-RA significance test applies (Reg (EU) 402/2013, KB-REG-CSM-RA).
Risk acceptance demonstrated via reference systems (a comparable re-signalled interlocking elsewhere on the network) plus explicit risk estimation for the degraded-mode transition.
An independent AsBo (Assessment Body) will assess the correct application of CSM-RA for the re-signalling change.

rows: Overall accountability for the application and the authorisation
Competence & Sentinel assurance for safety-critical roles
sections.caption: Accountabilities — role / accountability / status (named individuals de-identified to role labels)

sections.text: Competence and Sentinel currency for safety-critical roles, and the Entity in Charge of Maintenance arrangements for the infrastructure, are assured via the SMS and are cited by reference. Individual fitness-for-duty / medical detail is held in a separate confidential occupational-health record, not in this application.

The dutyholder's declaration confirms the SMS is in place and the application elements are complete; the package is assembled for submission to ORR. The current status is reported as a fact (not yet submitted) — it does not assert an ORR decision.

rows.action: Complete the AsBo appointment and the explicit-risk-estimation pack for the re-signalling change before submission

sections.text: This application is assembled FOR submission to ORR. ORR is the Safety Authority; granting the authorisation is its act and is not asserted in this document.

items: Railways and Other Guided Transport Systems (Safety) Regulations 2006 (SI 2006/599) — infrastructure manager → safety authorisation — KB-REG-ROGS
Common Safety Method for Risk Evaluation and Assessment, Reg (EU) 402/2013 (retained EU law in GB) — KB-REG-CSM-RA
Decision-support only — a competent person (rail-SMS / ORR-aware) must review before use.
